loanAssumptions.rateMethod: Uses SBA variable-rate ceilings by loan size as a conservative estimate.

candidates.name: J Chester & Associates CPA Firm
candidates.notes: Public team page shows multiple staff; tax, accounting, payroll mix.

underwriting_details.why_it_fits: High-fit accounting target with recurring client relationships, visible staff, and enough cash flow to matter.
revenue_and_cash_flow_view: Initial take: cash flow is in-range and structurally more attractive than a purely project-led service business.
underwriting_details.key_risks: Tax practice cyclicality, partner dependence, and client concentration need validation.
financing_view: Preliminary view: likely worth exploring if owner motivation and true transferability check out.
missing_facts: Is the owner actively retiring, and what is the exact timeline?
How many full-time employees are in place, and who runs day-to-day operations?
What percentage of revenue is contract or recurring versus one-off work?
What are the top five customers and their share of revenue?
How much of the current cash flow depends on the owner personally?
Is seller financing available and on what terms?
